title: journal-elsevier
description: Proves WasmTeX typesets an article against a caller-SUPPLIED top-journal class (Elsevier's elsarticle) that WasmTeX does NOT bundle: elsarticle.cls is a verbatim TeX Live file placed in the job's file set, not shipped in a tier. elsarticle's own \RequirePackages pull in recommended LaTeX packages that live in the on-demand `academic` tier, so the §5.4(a) static-scan mounts `academic` before pass 1 (resolution:scan, no 'file not found'); the article then typesets in one engine pass. TEST-ONLY capability check (compile-against-supplied-class); journal-class SHIPPING is deferred to a future milestone as a SEPARATE package, never folded into academic. The .tex is ORIGINAL work (not an Elsevier-supplied sample); the .cls is third-party LPPL-1.3 (see conformance/README.md + THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md).
